paper_title: Random Cayley graphs and random sumsets
summary: Conjecture 15 of arXiv:2509.02561 proposes an optimal covering lemma: collections \mathcal{F}_{\ell} of complexity \log|\mathcal{F}_{\ell}|=\tilde{O}(2^{\ell}) and minimum member size \tilde{\Omega}(2^{\ell}s) such that every set A with |A|=s and |A+A|\leq K|A| has its sumset containing some F\in\mathcal{F}_{\ell} for \ell\leq\log_2 K. The paper establishes a weaker version (Theorem 6) with complexity exponential in 2^{2\ell} rather than 2^{\ell}, and the conjecture asks to close this gap. No follow-up resolving the conjecture was found; the paper was posted in September 2025 and the conjecture appears to remain open.

notes: No follow-up found in indexed literature. The conjecture is an optimal strengthening of Theorem 6 of the same paper, reducing the complexity bound from \exp(O(2^{2\ell})) to \exp(\tilde{O}(2^{\ell})). It is motivated by Lovett's question (from Green's open problems list) on whether sumsets A+A of dense sets contain large subsets of the form B+B+B+B. The paper is very recent (September 2025), so the open status is expected.
